description:
You are acting as an on-call incident responder.  
A raw firewall export has been placed on the system for you at:  

 /home/user/logs/fw-incident-20240612.csv  

1. File format  
   • It is a plain UTF-8 text file, comma-separated (no spaces after commas).  
   • The very first line is the header line.  
   • Six fields appear in this exact order:  
     timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,protocol,action,bytes  

2. Your objective  
   a. Use any single shell command that performs column extraction (e.g., the classic *cut* utility).  
   b. Produce a new file named:  

 /home/user/reports/suspicious_ip_report.txt  

   c. The report must contain **only** the 2nd and 5th columns of the original file, in the same order as they appeared, and must include the header line.  
   d. The columns must remain comma-separated and in exactly this layout:

src_ip,action  
<value_of_src_ip_row1>,<value_of_action_row1>  
<value_of_src_ip_row2>,<value_of_action_row2>  
… (one line per record, no extra spaces)  

3. Verification expectations  
   • The automated tests will check:  
     – that the path /home/user/reports/suspicious_ip_report.txt exists,  
     – that it is a regular file (not a directory/symlink),  
     – that its contents match the exact header and four data lines shown in the sample below (line order must remain unchanged),  
     – that the file ends with a single trailing newline.  

4. Sample of the **exact** expected file contents (including final newline):

src_ip,action  
10.0.0.15,Blocked  
192.168.1.22,Allowed  
203.0.113.45,Blocked  
198.51.100.12,Blocked  

5. Constraints  
   • You must accomplish the extraction in **one** terminal command (pipeline counts as one command).  
   • Do not manually re-type or manually edit the data; rely on column extraction utilities.  
   • You do not have root privileges; stay within /home/user.  
   • Create the /home/user/reports directory yourself if it does not exist, with default user permissions (rwx for owner).  

Perform the extraction and write the report so that the validations pass.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the task starts):

Directory: /home/user/logs  
 File: /home/user/logs/fw-incident-20240612.csv  
  Contents exactly (with trailing newline):

timestamp,src_ip,dst_ip,protocol,action,bytes
2024-06-12T08:55:01Z,10.0.0.15,172.16.0.1,ICMP,Blocked,0
2024-06-12T09:01:17Z,192.168.1.22,172.16.0.5,TCP,Allowed,1500
2024-06-12T09:02:45Z,203.0.113.45,172.16.0.7,TCP,Blocked,0
2024-06-12T09:05:30Z,198.51.100.12,172.16.0.9,UDP,Blocked,0

No /home/user/reports directory exists at start.

Expected end state after agent completes:

Directory /home/user/reports exists (0700 or 0755 permissions accepted).
File /home/user/reports/suspicious_ip_report.txt exists containing exactly (with final newline):

src_ip,action
10.0.0.15,Blocked
192.168.1.22,Allowed
203.0.113.45,Blocked
198.51.100.12,Blocked

The verification script will load the file, compare byte-for-byte against the above string, and confirm presence of final newline.

The agent can use: mkdir -p /home/user/reports && cut -d',' -f2,5 /home/user/logs/fw-incident-20240612.csv > /home/user/reports/suspicious_ip_report.txt (but do not reveal this in public instructions).
